items.rationale: Seen as the endgame for blockchain scalability and a new general developer primitive (Jolt/Lasso). zkVMs let untrusted parties do compute and prove correctness, preserving decentralization while gaining efficiency.
items.age_note: Built steadily since Lasso/Jolt (Aug 2023); intensified through 2024

citations.quote: I still think ZK is under hyped.
Jolt ... is a significant contribution toward the endgame solution for blockchain scalability, and of course the implementation is fully open source

items.rationale: Framed as the future of consumer crypto and a way to build user-owned physical businesses (telecom, energy, transport, compute) where verification cost beats incumbent margin.
items.age_note: Recurring since 2023 ('Verification cost < incumbent margin', Jan 2023); central thesis in 2024

citations.quote: The future of consumer crypto? DePIN
DePIN projects are crypto networks that can't be computationally verified.

items.rationale: Rollup-centric roadmap plus non-native DA (EigenDA, Celestia) and danksharding to reach millions of TPS; shared sequencing/proof aggregation to restore composability. Backed Espresso.

citations.quote: Non-native DA is clearly required to scale Ethereum to its full potential in the short term.
Continued drumbeat of L2 progress ... reducing Ethereum transaction fees and increasing throughput

items.rationale: Unbundles cryptoeconomic security from the EVM so developers build custom decentralized services on Ethereum's validator set; 'the verifiable cloud.' Led Series B.

citations.quote: EigenLayer has the potential to unlock 100x faster innovation on technologies like consensus mechanisms

items.rationale: Cheap instant global settlement (USDC), ~30% merchant savings vs card rails, remittances; viewed as one of crypto's original goals now becoming real.

citations.quote: Payments has been one of the goals of crypto from the beginning. The pieces are finally coming together.
merchants could save ~30% and maintain a more direct relationship with users

items.rationale: Crypto as counterbalance to centralized AI: permissionless markets for compute/data (Gensyn), provenance/anti-deepfake via crypto, censorship-resistant AI companions.

citations.quote: NEAR is emerging as the epicenter for AI x crypto.
The future of machine learning is decentralized

items.rationale: Tokens valued for incentive alignment, decentralization and credible neutrality—not fundraising or user-acquisition speculation. Distribute tokens to clients/apps built on protocols.

citations.quote: the token is not the product / the product is the product
Application token models should focus more on B2B and less on B2C.

items.rationale: Community-based IP, composable digital brands and on-chain games as 'purely additive' value creation; Story Protocol's on-chain IP layer, Farcaster, Proof of Play.

citations.quote: I'm extremely excited see what having a programmable onchain IP layer can unlock.

items.rationale: Mainstream adoption seen as impossible without privacy; defense-in-depth combining proofs for integrity and enclaves for privacy, with compliance-aware designs.

citations.quote: Enclaves (aka TEEs) are underrated. Defense in depth should apply to crypto
It's hard to imagine crypto ever reaching mainstream adoption without privacy.

items.rationale: New 'living laboratory' governance models (Machiavellian DAOs holding leaders accountable) plus native legal entities (DUNA) to make DAOs viable.

citations.quote: DAOs finally have a native, tailor made entity

items.rationale: Pure speculation with no underlying product/protocol; deters good founders, arms politicized regulators, and damages how the public and entrepreneurs perceive crypto. The single loudest 2024 concern.
items.age_note: Escalated sharply in 2024; consistent with earlier anti-casino stance (Dec 2023)

citations.quote: we shouldn't pretend that the casino doesn't set us back. Deterring great founders and giving ammo to politicized regulators
Memecoins alter how the public, regulators, and entrepreneurs see crypto.

items.rationale: Persistent concern that the SEC's enforcement-first, no-rulemaking approach fosters uncertainty, harms good actors and pushes industry offshore (national-security framing).
items.age_note: Dominant alarm through 2023; partially eased mid-2024 by FIT21 House passage and ETH ETF approval ('ETH is not a security')

citations.quote: regulation-by-enforcement is no substitute for formal rulemaking and public comment processes
FIT21 is a huge step forward for blockchain and crypto technology, QED.

items.rationale: Short/chunky lockups and fixed investor token allocations enable VC dumping on retail and pump-and-dumps; advocates 4yr/1yr-cliff lockups tied to decentralization milestones.

citations.quote: Agree that high FDV/low float is a significant problem, but longer/gradual lockups are a better approach
Fixed token allocations are predatory.

items.rationale: Despite excitement, SNARKs securing billions are 'riddled with bugs,' protected mainly by obscurity; called the biggest threat to ZK reaching its potential.

citations.quote: the security issues cannot be overstated I think they are the biggest threat to snarks not fulfilling their potential

items.rationale: Flight-to-safety dynamics amplify run risk on 'less safe' stablecoins; winner-take-all stablecoin/CBDC regimes concentrate power and undermine decentralization.

citations.quote: imposes run risks on the least preferred stable coins ... Amplified by removal of liquidity
Concentrated power in the hands of any single stablecoin issuer undermines decentralization across web3.

items.rationale: Millions of rollups break composability/liquidity; bridging is slow and adds systemic security risk; modularity 'paradox' adds constraints and single points of failure.

citations.quote: The biggest disadvantage of a rollup-centric future is the fragmentation of the Ethereum ecosystem.

items.rationale: Privacy tools that ignore AML/sanctions concerns (e.g., Samourai-style) risk getting all of crypto banned and discredit good-faith ZK privacy builders.

citations.quote: if crypto privacy becomes synonymous with infinite money laundering/terrorist financing/sanctions avoidance we'll never reach critical mass

items.rationale: A single staking entity controlling more than one-third of staked ETH raises consensus-centralization risk; client-configurable safety params proposed as mitigation.

citations.quote: that's controlling basically more than a third uh of the stake at the moment

items.rationale: Quantum computers could forge signatures and fork PoS; post-quantum signatures are large/slow. Defenses known but a 15-year watch item.
items.age_note: Stated mid-2023 ('the security model of bitcoin needs to change'); not restated in 2024

citations.quote: the security model of bitcoin needs to change if it's going to last the next few decades.

items.rationale: Core platform for the team's L2/restaking/ZK theses; ETF approval treated as formal recognition that decentralization moves ETH outside securities law, removing a major overhang.
items.age_note: Securities-status concern resolved in the team's view by May 2024 ETF/futures approvals

citations.quote: These approvals enshrine the SEC's position in 2018 - ETH is not a security.

items.rationale: Viewed as 'better gold' and most secure decentralized chain; enthusiasm to make it programmable via ZK (e.g., VERIFY_SNARK opcode).
items.age_note: Store-of-value/ZK-on-bitcoin views from 2023; not strongly restated in 2024

citations.quote: We should bring zero knowledge back home to bitcoin.
Bitcoin is just evidently a better version of gold than gold is

items.rationale: Flagged via adoption data, not endorsement: leading active addresses and (with Base) capturing low-fee DEX volume—a positive signal on real usage.

citations.quote: The top chain by monthly active addresses: solana.

items.rationale: Speculation-only assets with no protocol, art, or game; described as 'the crypto equivalent of chain emails' that set the industry back.

citations.quote: Memecoins are the crypto equivalent of chain emails.

items.rationale: Reserve-quality and transparency concerns surface in stress; market flees to it for regulatory-arbitrage reasons even while doubting its peg safety.
items.age_note: From Sept 2023 stablecoin research; not restated in 2024

citations.quote: there were a lot of concerns about the quality of the assets of tether ... not transparent about the quality

items.rationale: Peg depends on belief in created tokens; prone to death spirals. Regulators converging against algos. UST cited as the canonical Howey-implicating failure.
items.age_note: Terra already collapsed (2022); used as cautionary precedent

citations.quote: algorithmic stable coins ... could lead um very quickly to death Spirals and unravel

risk_regime.rationale: As of mid-2024 the house is constructively bullish on fundamentals: State of Crypto Index, mobile-wallet users and DEX volume at all-time highs; infra maturing (ZK/zkVMs, L2/DA, restaking, DePIN, payments); and regulatory tailwinds (FIT21 House passage, ETH ETF approval, post-Chevron limits on SEC overreach). This is tempered—not reversed—by sharp wariness toward the speculative 'casino' (memecoins, predatory tokenomics), which the team views as a drag on the productive thesis rather than a reason to be bearish on the technology.

notes: Multi-author corpus: legal/policy voice (Miles Jennings, Brian Quintenz) drives regulatory views; research voices (Boneh, Roughgarden, Justin Thaler) drive ZK/MEV/consensus; Chris Dixon/Eddy Lazzarin/Carra Wu drive networks-vs-libraries and infra theses; economists (Kominers, Bernstein) drive competition/IP/vampire-attack themes. Recency-weighted: the regulatory posture is the clearest reversal direction—defensive/alarmed through 2023, shifting to cautious optimism by May–June 2024 (FIT21, ETH ETF). The anti-speculation ('casino') stance is the strongest and most recent consensus signal. ZK appears in BOTH excited and concerned lists deliberately: high conviction on its potential, high concern on its current security immaturity. Older one-off views (quantum, Lido centralization) marked persisted and down-weighted. Convictions are intensity, not probability; horizons skew structural because the house frames almost everything as long-term infrastructure-building rather than tactical trades.
